title: REVOLUTION SUMMER LEAGUE | WEEK 1 RECAP
content: Week 1 delivered a sizzling start to the Revolution Summer League. Tavarius Vance erupted for 35 points in a losing effort; Dennis Schut carried Purple with a 32-point takeover; Tommy Brockmyre and Matt Spear combined for 50 points to push Gray past Red; Blue survived a furious Black comeback by the slimmest of margins; and Pink jumped out to a 19-point first-half lead it never surrendered. Let's get into the action.

games.text: Purple came out on point. Behind a barrage of threes, Purple raced to a double-digit halftime lead. Dennis Schut was nearly unguardable, knocking down shot after shot on his way to a monster scoring night, while Dylan Pratico and Kevin Garrison chipped in on the glass to keep Green off balance. Green struggled to find any rhythm offensively in the opening frame, shooting cold from the field as Purple's defense forced tough looks all night. Halftime: Purple 37, Green 23. Green refused to go quietly. The second half belonged to Green, who found their footing and outscored Purple 39-35 over the final twenty minutes. Calder Horowitz-McCadden and Nick Lynn each poured in 18 points and controlled the boards, clawing the deficit back down possession by possession. But the hole dug in the first half proved too deep to climb out of. Purple won, 72-62.

performers.Purple: Dennis Schut — 32 pts, 11 REB; Dylan Pratico — 14 pts, 8 REB, 5 AST; Kevin Garrison — 5 pts, 11 REB, 4 AST
performers.Green: Calder Horowitz-McCadden — 18 pts, 18 REB; Nick Lynn — 18 pts, 14 REB; Aaron West — 11 pts, 9 REB

games.text: Gray had a chip on its shoulder. Red jumped out early and controlled the first half, using Chris Cayole's scoring punch to build an advantage. Jaren Jeffcoat cleaned the glass all half, and Red's defense kept Gray searching for answers early. It looked like Red might run away with things heading into the break. Halftime: Red 40, Gray 25. Gray had other plans. Tommy Brockmyre and Matt Spear caught fire together in the second half and dragged Gray back into the fight. Kerry Lyons chipped in on the boards while the duo kept attacking the rim. What started as a double-digit deficit turned into a dogfight that spilled into overtime. Gray came out on top in the extra period. Gray won, 76-73.

performers.Gray: Tommy Brockmyre — 27 pts, 7 REB, 3 AST; Matt Spear — 23 pts, 10 REB; Kerry Lyons — 6 pts, 10 REB
performers.Red: Chris Cayole — 21 pts, 9 REB; Jaren Jeffcoat — 15 pts, 15 REB; Will Oboh — 14 pts, 11 REB

games.text: Black wanted to make a statement early. Blue answered instead, building a surprising 43-34 lead at the half thanks to balanced scoring from Logan Letourneau, Brody Lathrop, and Drew Pelkey. Black's offense leaned heavily on one player in the first twenty minutes, and the rest of the roster couldn't get untracked against a Blue defense that closed off driving lanes. Halftime: Blue 43, Black 34. Black wasn't finished. Tavarius Vance took over in the second half, torching the nets for a large share of his 35-point night and single-handedly willing Black back into contention. The comeback fell just short, though, as Blue's early cushion held up by the narrowest of margins. Blue won, 73-71.

performers.Black: Tavarius Vance — 35 pts, 13 REB, 3 AST; Will Ameden — 13 pts, 7 REB
performers.Blue: Logan Letourneau — 22 pts, 6 AST; Brody Lathrop — 19 pts, 3 AST; Drew Pelkey — 13 pts, 12 REB

games.text: Pink meant business from the opening tip. Tavian Barrino was locked in from the jump, powering Pink to a commanding lead by halftime. Matt Riordan crashed the boards relentlessly, and White simply couldn't find an answer for Pink's scoring flurries in the first half. Halftime: Pink 37, White 18. White clawed back, but time ran short. Tyrone Conley and Wyatt Messier led a spirited rally in the second half and made things interesting late. It wasn't enough to erase the early damage, though, as Pink closed out a win to open the season. Pink won, 67-57.

performers.Pink: Tavian Barrino — 32 pts, 9 REB; Matt Riordan — 13 pts, 13 REB; Donnie Dwyer — 10 pts, 8 REB, 4 AST
performers.White: Tyrone Conley — 15 pts, 12 REB; Wyatt Messier — 14 pts, 5 REB; Aid Semic — 8 pts, 6 REB

sponsors.name: Alta Group & The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub
sponsors.message: A big thank you to our Week 1 sponsors, the Alta Group and The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub, for making this league possible.

sponsors.name: Warden's Wisdom
sponsors.message: This recap is brought to you by Warden's Wisdom, a mindfulness-based coaching business helping individuals relieve anxiety through personalized mindfulness practices.
sponsors.quote: Stillness isn't the absence of movement — it's the choice of presence.

closing: See you back here next week for more action from the Revolution Summer League.
